title: Arrow Data
documentation:
## Purpose
Apache Arrow defines an in-memory columnar format and an on-disk IPC file format (also called Feather v2). The [Arrow Data](https://capdag.com/browse/media:arrow;list;record) media type represents files written in the Arrow IPC file format, which stores an ordered list of typed record batches.

## When to use
Use this type when low-latency data exchange between nodes running in the same process or over shared memory matters, or when you need zero-copy reads into data-science tooling. Arrow is preferable over Parquet when compression overhead is undesirable and when the consumer will process data immediately rather than storing it long term.

## Notes
Arrow IPC files begin with a magic number and contain one or more record batches preceded by a schema message. The `.feather` extension refers to the same format. Implementers should use the Apache Arrow libraries; the PyArrow `ipc.open_file` API is the standard entry point for readers.
